groups.context_description: Term names justice as the defining inner character of God — the disposition of right dealing that flows from who God is

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 32:4 “The Rock , his work is perfect , for all his ways are justice . A God of faithfulness and without iniquity , just and upright is he .
Isa 5:16 But the Lord of hosts is exalted in justice , and the Holy God shows himself holy in righteousness .

groups.context_description: Term names justice as the inner moral orientation of the person — the disposition to seek, do, and love what is right in relation to others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 29:14 I put on righteousness , and it clothed me; my justice was like a robe and a turban .
Mic 6:8 He has told you , O man , what is good ; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice , and to love kindness , and to walk humbly with your God ?

groups.context_description: Term names the inner cry for justice — the longing of the person denied their right, reaching toward God for vindication

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 19:7 Behold , I cry out , ‘ Violence !’ but I am not answered ; I call for help , but there is no justice .
Hab 1:4 So the law is paralyzed , and justice never goes forth . For the wicked surround the righteous ; so justice goes forth perverted .

groups.context_description: Term names divine judgment as the event before which the inner person stands in appeal — the plea-context of God's judicial acts

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 20:9 ‘ If disaster comes upon us, the sword , judgment , or pestilence , or famine , we will stand before this house and before you— for your name is in this house —and cry out to you in our affliction , and you will hear and save .’

groups.context_description: Term names God's judicial acts as the consequence flowing from inner moral failure — the execution of judgment that reflects the inner state and conduct of those judged

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 19:29 Condemnation is ready for scoffers , and beating for the backs of fools .

groups.context_description: Term names the condition of the inner person whose own beliefs or conduct become the instrument of their condemnation — the conscience turned against the self

anchor_verses.verse_text: Tit 3:11 knowing that such a person is warped and sinful ; he is self-condemned .

groups.context_description: Term describes condemnation as the verdict passed on the inner person on the basis of their inner state — unbelief, doubt, or moral failure becoming the grounds of condemnation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 16:16 Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved , but whoever does not believe will be condemned .
Rom 14:23 But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats , because the eating is not from faith . For whatever does not proceed from faith is sin .

groups.context_description: Term names the freedom from condemnation — the security of the inner person before God when condemnation is withheld or overcome

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 8:11 She said , “ No one , Lord .” And Jesus said , “ Neither do I condemn you ; go , and from now on sin no more .” ]]
Rom 8:34 Who is to condemn ? Christ Jesus is the one who died — more than that , who was raised — who is at the right hand of God , who indeed is interceding for us .

groups.context_description: Term names condemnation as an inner state produced by the law's verdict — the weight of moral judgment falling on the conscience, contrasted with the freedom of righteousness

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 3:9 For if there was glory in the ministry of condemnation , the ministry of righteousness must far exceed it in glory .

groups.context_description: Term names judgment as the coming event that orients and shapes the inner life — the fearful expectation of divine verdict that calls for moral accountability and reverence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 9:27 And just as it is appointed for man to die once , and after that comes judgment ,
Heb 10:27 but a fearful expectation of judgment , and a fury of fire that will consume the adversaries .

groups.context_description: Term names the inward parts as the seat of the innermost self — the deepest layer of the person's inner life that God searches, tests, and knows

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 139:13 For you formed my inward parts ; you knitted me together in my mother’s womb .
Jer 17:10 “ I the Lord search the heart and test the mind , to give every man according to his ways , according to the fruit of his deeds .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inward parts as the seat of inner experience — the deepest place in the person where joy, pain, instruction, and affliction are felt

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 16:7 I bless the Lord who gives me counsel ; in the night also my heart instructs me.
Pro 23:16 My inmost being will exult when your lips speak what is right .

groups.context_description: Term names a conscious inner awareness — moral complicity or cognitive recognition — that informs volitional response

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 5:2 and with his wife’s knowledge he kept back for himself some of the proceeds and brought only a part of it and laid it at the apostles ’ feet .
Act 12:12 When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary , the mother of John whose other name was Mark , where many were gathered together and were praying .

groups.context_description: Covetousness as the insatiable inner orientation toward possessing more

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Pe 2:14 They have eyes full of adultery , insatiable for sin . They entice unsteady souls . They have hearts trained in greed . Accursed children !

groups.context_description: Covetousness as idolatry — the ultimate inner-being misalignment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 3:5 Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality , impurity , passion , evil desire , and covetousness , which is idolatry .

groups.context_description: The inner state of discouragement — loss of heart produced by provocation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 3:21 Fathers , do not provoke your children , lest they become discouraged .

groups.context_description: Inner courage/cheerfulness grounded in faith amid crisis

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 27:25 So take heart , men , for I have faith in God that it will be exactly as I have been told .

groups.context_description: Cheerfulness as the inner-being ground of praise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 5:13 Is anyone among you suffering ? Let him pray . Is anyone cheerful ? Let him sing praise .

groups.context_description: Cheerful inner composure and confidence in adversarial or crisis contexts

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 24:10 And when the governor had nodded to him to speak , Paul replied : “ Knowing that for many years you have been a judge over this nation , I cheerfully make my defense .

groups.context_description: Eagerness as the inner disposition of forward-leaning readiness toward God and good

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 17:11 Now these Jews were more noble than those in Thessalonica ; they received the word with all eagerness , examining the Scriptures daily to see if these things were so .

groups.context_description: The willing spirit — inner eagerness toward God in tension with fleshly weakness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 26:41 Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation . The spirit indeed is willing , but the flesh is weak .”

groups.context_description: Eagerness as the inner motivation for mission

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:15 So I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome .

groups.context_description: Eager willing service — inner orientation of freedom and genuine care contrasted with compulsion and greed

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 5:2 shepherd the flock of God that is among you , exercising oversight , not under compulsion , but willingly , as God would have you; not for shameful gain , but eagerly ;

groups.context_description: Term names disobedience as the inner condition and defining orientation of the person before God — the state of being characterised by active resistance or refusal; the inner disposition from which the wrath of God follows

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 2:2 in which you once walked , following the course of this world , following the prince of the power of the air , the spirit that is now at work in the sons of disobedience —

groups.context_description: Term names disobedience as the inner failure that excludes from rest — the disposition of the heart that prevents entry into what God offers; disobedience as spiritual self-exclusion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 4:6 Since therefore it remains for some to enter it , and those who formerly received the good news failed to enter because of disobedience ,

groups.context_description: Term names disobedience as the defining inner act with moral and covenantal consequence — the primal refusal that constituted the human condition; the act or disposition of disobedience that draws retribution and demands correction

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:19 For as by the one man’s disobedience the many were made sinners , so by the one man’s obedience the many will be made righteous .

groups.context_description: Term names the wilful refusal to hear — the inner orientation of closed attention or contempt expressed through the repeated failure to receive the voice of the community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 18:17 If he refuses to listen to them , tell it to the church . And if he refuses to listen even to the church , let him be to you as a Gentile and a tax collector .

groups.context_description: Term names greed as an inner-being condition of the person — the inner fullness of rapacious desire that contradicts external religious propriety, and whose opposite is the inner freedom that accepts loss with joy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:25 “ Woe to you , scribes and Pharisees , hypocrites ! For you clean the outside of the cup and the plate , but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence .

groups.context_description: Term names the snatching that threatens or violates the inner being — the evil one taking the word from the heart, the wolf scattering the sheep, and the divine security that makes such snatching impossible

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 13:19 When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what has been sown in his heart . This is what was sown along the path .
Joh 10:29 My Father , who has given them to me , is greater than all , and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father’s hand .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine catching away of the person — eschatological rapture, Spirit transportation, and protective snatching to God — acts in which the inner person is taken into the divine presence or safety

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Th 4:17 Then we who are alive , who are left , will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air , and so we will always be with the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the greedy person as a moral-character type — the inner disposition of insatiable acquisitive desire that constitutes idolatry, excludes from the kingdom, and disqualifies from community in Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 5:5 For you may be sure of this , that everyone who is sexually immoral or impure , or who is covetous ( that is , an idolater ), has no inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being event of incurring and realising guilt — the moment when a person becomes guilty before God and the recognition of that guilt that precedes confession and atonement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 5:5 when he realizes his guilt in any of these and confesses the sin he has committed,

groups.context_description: Term names the bearing of guilt and its communal consequences — the guilt that brings judgment on individuals and nations who persist in sin, including inner acknowledgement and confession

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 5:15 I will return again to my place , until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face , and in their distress earnestly seek me .

groups.context_description: Term names the guilt offering as the divinely instituted response to guilt — the atonement mechanism that addresses the inner-being condition of guilt, supremely fulfilled in the Servant's soul as the guilt offering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:10 Yet it was the will of the Lord to crush him; he has put him to grief ; when his soul makes an offering for guilt , he shall see his offspring ; he shall prolong his days ; the will of the Lord shall prosper in his hand .

groups.context_description: Term names the state of being guilty — the inner recognition of guilt named by the person themselves, including the communal conscience that speaks before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 42:21 Then they said to one another , “In truth we are guilty concerning our brother , in that we saw the distress of his soul , when he begged us and we did not listen . That is why this distress has come upon us.”

groups.context_description: Term names guiltiness as the accumulated inner-spiritual condition of a person or community before God — the weight of guilt that accumulates, is confessed, and is addressed through atonement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 9:6 saying : “O my God , I am ashamed and blush to lift my face to you, my God , for our iniquities have risen higher than our heads , and our guilt has mounted up to the heavens .

groups.context_description: Term names iniquity as the inner-being condition of moral debt before God — the accumulated perversion of the human heart that God marks, bears, forgives, and will ultimately remember no more

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:6 All we like sheep have gone astray ; we have turned —every one —to his own way ; and the Lord has laid on him the iniquity of us all .

Jer 31:34 And no longer shall each one teach his neighbor and each his brother , saying , ‘ Know the Lord ,’ for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest , declares the Lord . For I will forgive their iniquity , and I will remember their sin no more .”

groups.context_description: Term names the personal confession of sin before God — the inner turning point at which the sinner names their sin, takes ownership, and turns toward God in acknowledgement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:4 Against you, you only , have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight , so that you may be justified in your words and blameless in your judgment .
Psa 119:11 I have stored up your word in my heart , that I might not sin against you .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the sustained inner-being orientation away from God — the pattern of covenantal unfaithfulness characterising individuals and generations who walk in idolatry

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 17:7 And this occurred because the people of Israel had sinned against the Lord their God , who had brought them up out of the land of Egypt from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt , and had feared other gods

groups.context_description: Term names the moral wisdom about sin — the universal reality that no one is without sin, the consequences sin brings, and the inner posture that safeguards against it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who does good and never sins .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the offence and burden carried by persons and peoples before God — the sin confessed, forgiven, covered, borne, and purged through divine grace

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:5 Behold , I was brought forth in iniquity , and in sin did my mother conceive me.
Isa 1:18 “ Come now , let us reason together, says the Lord : though your sins are like scarlet , they shall be as white as snow ; though they are red like crimson , they shall become like wool .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the named offence confessed to and before God — the great sin acknowledged and the prayer for covering and forgiveness that follows

anchor_verses.verse_text: A Maskil of David . Psa 32:1 Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven , whose sin is covered .

groups.context_description: Term names the moral ground of a person's standing — the charge, reason, or basis for guilt or innocence, including the declared innocence of Christ and the inner conviction that grounds Paul's suffering without shame

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 19:4 Pilate went out again and said to them , “ See , I am bringing him out to you that you may know that I find no guilt in him .”

groups.context_description: Term names the causal ground of guilt or salvation — the found absence of guilt in Christ and his being the source of eternal salvation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 5:9 And being made perfect , he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him ,

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as inner moral witness — it bears testimony, accuses, or excuses the person regarding their conduct before God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 23:1 And looking intently at the council , Paul said , “ Brothers , I have lived my life before God in all good conscience up to this day .”
Rom 2:15 They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts , while their conscience also bears witness , and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even excuse them

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as morally vulnerable — susceptible to defilement, weakening through wrong associations, or cauterisation by persistent sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:7 However , not all possess this knowledge . But some , through former association with idols , eat food as really offered to an idol , and their conscience , being weak , is defiled .
1Ti 4:2 through the insincerity of liars whose consciences are seared ,

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as the interior moral foundation of godly living — holding faith and conduct with a clear or good conscience

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 13:5 Therefore one must be in subjection , not only to avoid God’s wrath but also for the sake of conscience .
1Ti 1:5 The aim of our charge is love that issues from a pure heart and a good conscience and a sincere faith .

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as the object of Christ's redemptive purification — what the old sacrificial system could not cleanse, his blood can

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ , who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God , purify our conscience from dead works to serve the living God .
Heb 10:22 let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith , with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the guilt and offence before God that requires forgiveness — the relational breach that Christ came to address, that is confessed, atoned for, and forgiven through his blood

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 1:21 She will bear a son , and you shall call his name Jesus , for he will save his people from their sins .”
2Cor 5:21 For our sake he made him to be sin who knew no sin , so that in him we might become the righteousness of God .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as an indwelling power that rules, enslaves, and deceives the inner being — the force that reigns in the mortal body, dwells within the person, and wages war against the law of the mind

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 8:34 Jesus answered them , “ Truly , truly , I say to you , everyone who practices sin is a slave to sin .
Rom 7:17 So now it is no longer I who do it , but sin that dwells within me .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the universal human condition and objective state of all persons apart from Christ — the condition that defines, condemns, and is overcome only by the righteousness of God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 16:8 And when he comes , he will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment :
Rom 3:9 What then ? Are we Jews any better off? No , not at all . For we have already charged that all, both Jews and Greeks , are under sin ,

groups.context_description: Term names the physical structure of justice — the hall where right judgment is pronounced, embodying the inner commitment to justice in institutional form

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 7:7 And he made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, even the Hall of Judgment . It was finished with cedar from floor to rafters .

groups.context_description: Term names the hall of royal authority — the place where sovereign power and its inner orientation toward justice are made manifest

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 7:7 And he made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, even the Hall of Judgment . It was finished with cedar from floor to rafters .

groups.context_description: Soul's orientation toward God's mishpat — longing, delight, fear, praise, hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:20 My soul is consumed with longing for your rules at all times .
Psa 119:120 My flesh trembles for fear of you, and I am afraid of your judgments .

groups.context_description: Moral-spiritual orientation of the heart — faithfulness, rebellion, or Spirit-enabled transformation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 26:16 “ This day the Lord your God commands you to do these statutes and rules . You shall therefore be careful to do them with all your heart and with all your soul .
Eze 36:27 And I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes and be careful to obey my rules .

groups.context_description: Inner moral standard for human judgement — impartiality, righteousness, God-accountability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 19:15 “You shall do no injustice in court . You shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great , but in righteousness shall you judge your neighbor .
2Ch 19:6 and said to the judges , “ Consider what you do , for you judge not for man but for the Lord . He is with you in giving judgment .

groups.context_description: Judicial arena where inner life meets divine justice — humility, complaint, vindication-hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 143:2 Enter not into judgment with your servant , for no one living is righteous before you.
Jer 12:1 Righteous are you , O Lord , when I complain to you; yet I would plead my case before you. Why does the way of the wicked prosper ? Why do all who are treacherous thrive ?

groups.context_description: Justice as spirit-given capacity for inner discernment and righteous action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 28:6 and a spirit of justice to him who sits in judgment , and strength to those who turn back the battle at the gate .

groups.context_description: Judging as requiring inner moral discernment — righteousness, impartiality, God-accountability in the judge

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 19:6 and said to the judges , “ Consider what you do , for you judge not for man but for the Lord . He is with you in giving judgment .
Isa 11:3 And his delight shall be in the fear of the Lord . He shall not judge by what his eyes see , or decide disputes by what his ears hear ,

groups.context_description: Inner appeal to God as judge — cry for divine vindication, justice, or moral verdict

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 18:25 Far be it from you to do such a thing , to put the righteous to death with the wicked , so that the righteous fare as the wicked ! Far be that from you! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just ?”
1Sa 24:12 May the Lord judge between me and you, may the Lord avenge me against you, but my hand shall not be against you .

groups.context_description: Divine judgement engaging inner moral condition — judged according to ways, wickedness, spiritual orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:4 Against you, you only , have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight , so that you may be justified in your words and blameless in your judgment .
Eze 18:30 “ Therefore I will judge you, O house of Israel , every one according to his ways , declares the Lord God . Repent and turn from all your transgressions , lest iniquity be your ruin .

groups.context_description: God as righteous judge whose inner character is expressed through just acts — indignation, equity, faithfulness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 7:11 God is a righteous judge , and a God who feels indignation every day .
Isa 33:22 For the Lord is our judge ; the Lord is our lawgiver ; the Lord is our king ; he will save us .

groups.context_description: Inner moral standing before God — being righteous, accounted righteous, or incapacity to be righteous

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:4 Against you, you only , have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight , so that you may be justified in your words and blameless in your judgment .

Isa 53:11 Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied ; by his knowledge shall the righteous one , my servant , make many to be accounted righteous , and he shall bear their iniquities .

groups.context_description: Judicial act of vindicating the righteous or condemning the wicked — requiring inner moral discernment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 25:1 “ If there is a dispute between men and they come into court and the judges decide between them, acquitting the innocent and condemning the guilty ,

1Ki 8:32 then hear in heaven and act and judge your servants , condemning the guilty by bringing his conduct on his own head , and vindicating the righteous by rewarding him according to his righteousness .

groups.context_description: Righteousness as defining inner quality of the person — moral character, integrity, standing before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 29:14 I put on righteousness , and it clothed me; my justice was like a robe and a turban .
Psa 7:8 The Lord judges the peoples ; judge me, O Lord , according to my righteousness and according to the integrity that is in me .

groups.context_description: Righteousness as attribute of God — character expressed in just acts, judgements, steadfast faithfulness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 89:14 Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne ; steadfast love and faithfulness go before you .

groups.context_description: Righteousness as inner orientation pursued and cultivated — active seeking, learning, walking in righteousness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 51:1 “ Listen to me, you who pursue righteousness , you who seek the Lord : look to the rock from which you were hewn , and to the quarry from which you were dug .
Isa 51:7 “ Listen to me, you who know righteousness , the people in whose heart is my law ; fear not the reproach of man , nor be dismayed at their revilings .

groups.context_description: Righteousness as moral basis for just governance — inner character required of kings, judges, and rulers

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 45:7 you have loved righteousness and hated wickedness . Therefore God , your God , has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions ;
Isa 11:5 Righteousness shall be the belt of his waist , and faithfulness the belt of his loins .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine act of judging — God's inner determination to vindicate his people, execute justice among the nations, and rule with righteousness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 32:36 For the Lord will vindicate his people and have compassion on his servants , when he sees that their power is gone and there is none remaining, bond or free .
Psa 72:2 May he judge your people with righteousness , and your poor with justice !

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of judging on behalf of the poor — the inner moral obligation to give right judgment to the vulnerable

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 31:9 Open your mouth , judge righteously , defend the rights of the poor and needy .
Jer 22:16 He judged the cause of the poor and needy ; then it was well . Is not this to know me? declares the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the cause or right of the person — the just claim that calls for inner moral commitment from the judge and from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 9:4 For you have maintained my just cause ; you have sat on the throne , giving righteous judgment.
Jer 22:16 He judged the cause of the poor and needy ; then it was well . Is not this to know me? declares the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names divine and judicial verdict — the authoritative inner determination of guilt or innocence pronounced by God or the court

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 36:17 “But you are full of the judgment on the wicked ; judgment and justice seize you.
Psa 76:8 From the heavens you uttered judgment ; the earth feared and was still ,

groups.context_description: Term names the judgment that determines the outcome for powers and persons — the authoritative verdict of God or the court that shapes inner destiny

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 7:22 until the Ancient of Days came , and judgment was given for the saints of the Most High , and the time came when the saints possessed the kingdom .
Dan 7:26 But the court shall sit in judgment, and his dominion shall be taken away , to be consumed and destroyed to the end .

groups.context_description: Term names God as the divine judge — the one whose inner justice and attentiveness to the wronged defines the nature of true judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 24:15 May the Lord therefore be judge and give sentence between me and you, and see to it and plead my cause and deliver me from your hand .”
Psa 68:5 Father of the fatherless and protector of widows is God in his holy habitation .

groups.context_description: Term names the act or condition of moral wrongdoing — the inner moral deficit that constitutes crime before God and human courts

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 18:14 But when Paul was about to open his mouth , Gallio said to the Jews , “ If it were a matter of wrongdoing or vicious crime , O Jews , I would have reason to accept your complaint.
Rev 18:5 for her sins are heaped high as heaven , and God has remembered her iniquities .

groups.context_description: Term characterises the person or action as morally unjust — the inner condition of unrighteousness that stands opposed to God and his standards

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:9 Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God ? Do not be deceived : neither the sexually immoral , nor idolaters , nor adulterers , nor men who practice homosexuality ,
1Pe 3:18 For Christ also suffered once for sins , the righteous for the unrighteous , that he might bring us to God , being put to death in the flesh but made alive in the spirit ,

groups.context_description: Term names the quality of enduring unjust suffering — the inner disposition of patient endurance before God when wronged

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 2:19 For this is a gracious thing , when , mindful of God , one endures sorrows while suffering unjustly .

groups.context_description: Term names the adversary in legal or spiritual contest — the opposition that calls forth inner urgency, persistence, or vigilance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 18:3 And there was a widow in that city who kept coming to him and saying , ‘Give me justice against my adversary .’
1Pe 5:8 Be sober-minded ; be watchful . Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion , seeking someone to devour .

groups.context_description: Term names the righteous judgment of God — the divine standard of just assessment that confronts the hard-hearted inner condition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:5 But because of your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God’s righteous judgment will be revealed .

groups.context_description: Term characterises the person as morally righteous — the inner quality of justice and uprightness that defines the person who stands rightly before God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 1:19 And her husband Joseph , being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame , resolved to divorce her quietly .
Jam 5:16 Therefore , confess your sins to one another and pray for one another , that you may be healed . The prayer of a righteous person has great power as it is working .

groups.context_description: Term characterises God or Christ as just and righteous — the divine inner attribute of perfect moral justice

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 17:25 O righteous Father , even though the world does not know you , I know you , and these know that you have sent me .
Rev 16:5 And I heard the angel in charge of the waters say , “ Just are you, O Holy One , who is and who was , for you brought these judgments .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine righteousness revealed and imputed — the righteousness of God that becomes the ground of the person's standing before him

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith for faith , as it is written , “The righteous shall live by faith .”
2Cor 5:21 For our sake he made him to be sin who knew no sin , so that in him we might become the righteousness of God .

groups.context_description: Term names human righteousness as an inner quality and lived conduct — the moral character and orientation of the person who lives rightly before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:6 “ Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness , for they shall be satisfied .
Heb 1:9 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness ; therefore God , your God , has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions .”

groups.context_description: Term names righteousness obtained and pursued by faith — the inner orientation of seeking righteousness through trust in God rather than works

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 9:30 What shall we say , then ? That Gentiles who did not pursue righteousness have attained it, that is , a righteousness that is by faith ;
Gal 5:5 For through the Spirit , by faith , we ourselves eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine act of justification — the declaration that transforms the person's inner moral standing before God, received through faith

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 3:24 and are justified by his grace as a gift, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus ,
Rom 5:1 Therefore , since we have been justified by faith , we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names the quality of acting or suffering in a morally right manner — the adverbial dimension of inner moral alignment expressed in conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 23:41 And we indeed justly , for we are receiving the due reward of our deeds ; but this man has done nothing wrong .”
Tit 2:12 training us to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions , and to live self-controlled , upright , and godly lives in the present age ,

groups.context_description: Term names justification as the act that restores right standing before God — the inner transformation of moral status through Christ's work

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 4:25 who was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification .
Rom 5:18 Therefore , as one trespass led to condemnation for all men , so one act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all men .

groups.context_description: Term names the human judge or ruler in the context of the inner act of rejecting divinely appointed authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 7:27 But the man who was wronging his neighbor thrust him aside , saying , ‘ Who made you a ruler and a judge over us ?
Act 7:35 “ This Moses , whom they rejected , saying , ‘ Who made you a ruler and a judge ?’—this man God sent as both ruler and redeemer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush .

groups.context_description: Term names the just punishment or condemnation that follows moral transgression — the consequence of the inner condition of wickedness before divine justice

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Th 1:9 They will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction , away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might ,

Jude 7 just as Sodom and Gomorrah and the surrounding cities , which likewise indulged in sexual immorality and pursued unnatural desire , serve as an example by undergoing a punishment of eternal fire .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner cry and petition for divine justice — the persistent appeal of the wronged person to God for vindication

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 18:3 And there was a widow in that city who kept coming to him and saying , ‘Give me justice against my adversary .’
Rev 6:10 They cried out with a loud voice , “O Sovereign Lord , holy and true , how long before you will judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the execution of divine vengeance and justice — God's inner determination to right wrongs and vindicate the oppressed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:19 Beloved , never avenge yourselves , but leave it to the wrath of God, for it is written , “ Vengeance is mine , I will repay , says the Lord .”
Rev 19:2 for his judgments are true and just ; for he has judged the great prostitute who corrupted the earth with her immorality , and has avenged on her the blood of his servants .”

groups.context_description: Term names the divine vengeance and justice that arises from God's inner righteousness in response to evil — and the human cry for such justice

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 18:7 And will not God give justice to his elect , who cry to him day and night ? , Will he delay long over them ?
Heb 10:30 For we know him who said , “ Vengeance is mine ; I will repay .” And again , “The Lord will judge his people .”

groups.context_description: Term names the avenging character of divine authority — the inner justice that punishes wrongdoing and protects the wronged

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 13:4 for he is God’s servant for your good . But if you do wrong , be afraid , for he does not bear the sword in vain . For he is the servant of God , an avenger who carries out God’s wrath on the wrongdoer .
1Th 4:6 that no one transgress and wrong his brother in this matter , because the Lord is an avenger in all these things , as we told you beforehand and solemnly warned you.

groups.context_description: Term names the just or righteous character of a consequence or penalty — the inner moral logic of appropriate retribution

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 3:8 And why not do evil that good may come ?— as some people slanderously charge us with saying . Their condemnation is just .
Heb 2:2 For since the message declared by angels proved to be reliable , and every transgression or disobedience received a just retribution ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of moral condemnation — the judgment that pronounces another guilty and the divine prohibition of such judgment by the unmerciful

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 12:37 for by your words you will be justified , and by your words you will be condemned .”
Luk 6:37 , “ Judge not , and you will not be judged ; condemn not , and you will not be condemned ; forgive , and you will be forgiven ;

groups.context_description: Term names the condition of moral accountability before God — the inner standing of being answerable to divine judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says it speaks to those who are under the law , so that every mouth may be stopped , and the whole world may be held accountable to God .

groups.context_description: Term names deliberate human action as the expression of inner character and will — what a person does as the outward enactment of their inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 56:1 Thus says the Lord : “ Keep justice , and do righteousness , for soon my salvation will come , and my righteousness be revealed .
Mic 6:8 He has told you , O man , what is good ; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice , and to love kindness , and to walk humbly with your God ?

groups.context_description: Term names the law or decree as the framework within which inner moral and religious commitment is tested — the external standard that reveals the inner orientation of the person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 7:26 Whoever will not obey the law of your God and the law of the king , let judgment be strictly executed on him , whether for death or for banishment or for confiscation of his goods or for imprisonment .”
Dan 6:5 Then these men said , “We shall not find any ground for complaint against this Daniel unless we find it in connection with the law of his God .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of perceiving or approving something as right — the moral/personal assessment that something accords with what is right and true

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 14:3 But his father and mother said to him, “Is there not a woman among the daughters of your relatives , or among all our people , that you must go to take a wife from the uncircumcised Philistines ?” But Samson said to his father , “ Get her for me, for she is right in my eyes .”
Psa 119:128 Therefore I consider all your precepts to be right ; I hate every false way .

groups.context_description: Term names the quality of moral uprightness — the straight inner path and conduct that reflects alignment with God's ways

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 5:8 Lead me, O Lord , in your righteousness because of my enemies ; make your way straight before me.
Hab 2:4 “ Behold , his soul is puffed up ; it is not upright within him, but the righteous shall live by his faith .

groups.context_description: Term names the quality of moral and spiritual uprightness — the inner straightness of the person who walks rightly before God and detests what perverts justice

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:21 Then I proclaimed a fast there , at the river Ahava , that we might humble ourselves before our God , to seek from him a safe journey for ourselves, our children , and all our goods .
Mic 3:9 Hear this , you heads of the house of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel , who detest justice and make crooked all that is straight ,

groups.context_description: Term names the perversion of justice — the inner moral distortion of a people that has abandoned divine standards and denies God's seeing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 9:9 Then he said to me, “The guilt of the house of Israel and Judah is exceedingly great . The land is full of blood , and the city full of injustice . For they say , ‘The Lord has forsaken the land , and the Lord does not see .’

groups.context_description: Term names reconciliation as the inner-relational restoration of broken relationship between God and humanity — the ending of enmity and establishment of peace at the relational core of personhood

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:18 All this is from God , who through Christ reconciled us to himself and gave us the ministry of reconciliation ;

groups.context_description: Term names the act of reconciliation — the active work of restoring broken inner-relational standing, whether between God and humanity or within human relationships

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:10 For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son , much more , now that we are reconciled , shall we be saved by his life .

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of comforting — bringing relief to inner grief through presence, words, or care

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 23:4 Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death , I will fear no evil , for you are with me; your rod and your staff , they comfort me .
Lam 1:16 “ For these things I weep ; my eyes flow with tears ; for a comforter is far from me, one to revive my spirit ; my children are desolate , for the enemy has prevailed .”

groups.context_description: Term names divine comfort — God actively bringing relief and restoration to the inner grief of his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 40:1 Comfort , comfort my people , says your God .
Isa 66:13 As one whom his mother comforts , so I will comfort you; you shall be comforted in Jerusalem .

groups.context_description: Term names human relenting — the inner change of heart toward repentance before God or reversal of a previous disposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 42:6 therefore I despise myself, and repent in dust and ashes .”

groups.context_description: Term names divine relenting — God's inner-being disposition changing in response to human repentance or intercession, turning from intended judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 32:14 And the Lord relented from the disaster that he had spoken of bringing on his people .
Jon 3:10 When God saw what they did , how they turned from their evil way , God relented of the disaster that he had said he would do to them, and he did not do it.

groups.context_description: Term names the withholding of divine compassion — the inner-relational disposition of God toward a people under judgment, where compassion is deliberately hidden

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 13:14 I shall ransom them from the power of Sheol ; I shall redeem them from Death . O Death , where are your plagues ? O Sheol , where is your sting ? Compassion is hidden from my eyes .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of recalling and turning to mind — deliberate inner movement of memory and reflection that leads to repentance, discernment, or hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lam 3:21 But this I call to mind , and therefore I have hope :

groups.context_description: Term names repentance as the inner act of turning toward God — the deliberate inner reorientation of the whole person from evil ways back to covenantal faithfulness, with all the heart and soul

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 33:11 Say to them, As I live , declares the Lord God , I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked , but that the wicked turn from his way and live ; turn back , turn back from your evil ways , for why will you die , O house of Israel ?

groups.context_description: Term names God's relenting as a divine inner-relational response to human repentance — God turning from judgment when his people turn, expressing the inner disposition of divine compassion and the withholding of wrath

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jon 3:10 When God saw what they did , how they turned from their evil way , God relented of the disaster that he had said he would do to them, and he did not do it.

groups.context_description: Term names the irrevocable quality of God's gifts and calling (grounding identity) and the quality of genuine repentance that produces no regret — immutability as a characteristic of both divine commitment and authentic inner transformation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 7:10 For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation without regret , whereas worldly grief produces death .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner reversal of a previous decision — the change of mind or feeling of regret that follows a prior action or stance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 27:3 Then when Judas , his betrayer , saw that Jesus was condemned , he changed his mind and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders ,

groups.context_description: Term names repentance as the inner-being act of turning — the complete reorientation of the person from sin toward God, called for by prophets, apostles, and Christ himself

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 1:15 and saying , “The time is fulfilled , and the kingdom of God is at hand ; repent and believe in the gospel .”

Act 26:20 but declared first to those in Damascus , then in Jerusalem and throughout all the region of Judea , and also to the Gentiles , that they should repent and turn to God , performing deeds in keeping with their repentance .

groups.context_description: Term names repentance as the inner-being transformation — the godly grief that leads to reorientation of life toward God, given as a divine gift and leading to forgiveness and life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 11:18 When they heard these things they fell silent . And they glorified God , saying , “ Then to the Gentiles also God has granted repentance that leads to life .”
2Cor 7:10 For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation without regret , whereas worldly grief produces death .
